jokes.text: The entire opening struggle to get the Christmas tree through the door - multiple people shouting contradictory instructions
jokes.setup: Simple task of bringing in a Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Complete chaos with everyone yelling different directions
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup with good escalation, though fairly standard sitcom fare

jokes.text: Ah, I got a splinter. / Well, suck it up. We all have problems.
jokes.setup: Someone gets hurt bringing in the tree
jokes.punchline: Dwight's characteristically unsympathetic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment - brutal efficiency and lack of empathy

jokes.text: That's what she said.
jokes.setup: Why did you get it so big?
jokes.punchline: Michael's signature sexual innuendo catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic Michael moment - predictable but perfectly executed and became cultural touchstone

jokes.text: And B: I wanted it to be impressive. The biggest day of the year deserves the biggest tree of the year.
jokes.setup: Someone questions why the tree is so big
jokes.punchline: Michael's grandiose justification for poor planning
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Michael's tendency to over-rationalize his mistakes

jokes.text: And we are going to sell that to charity. That's what Christmas is all about.
jokes.setup: What to do with the broken off part of the tree
jokes.punchline: Michael's twisted logic about selling garbage to charity being the Christmas spirit
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Michael's self-serving interpretation of generosity

jokes.text: Jim's entire talking head about the teapot gift with inside jokes for Pam
jokes.setup: Jim explains his Secret Santa gift
jokes.punchline: The elaborate thoughtfulness contrasted with silly inside jokes
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character development and relationship building through comedy - shows Jim's deep attention to Pam

jokes.text: She put this on a hot dog a couple years ago because she thought it was catsup. And it was really funny, so I kept the other two.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining the hot sauce packet
jokes.punchline: The absurd sweetness of keeping condiment packets as romantic mementos
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jim's romantic but dorky nature - genuinely touching and funny simultaneously

jokes.text: This would take a little too long to explain. So I won't.
jokes.setup: Jim showing another mysterious gift item
jokes.punchline: Deliberately withholding the explanation creates intrigue and humor
jokes.explanation: Clever writing technique - the mystery is funnier than any explanation could be

jokes.text: Do you think I should have gotten the big ones? / We'll see.
jokes.setup: Phyllis asking about Christmas light choice
jokes.punchline: Angela's characteristically cold, non-committal response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Angela's controlling, judgmental nature

jokes.text: Which, to me, seems... excessive.
jokes.setup: Angela's over-the-top party disaster preparation
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's mild understatement about Angela's controlling behavior
jokes.explanation: Nice understated humor showing the office dynamics through Phyllis's gentle criticism

jokes.text: Ho ho ho! Pimp! I'm kidding!
jokes.setup: Michael arriving as Santa
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate greeting immediately followed by backtracking
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael moment - inappropriate comment followed by weak attempt to cover

jokes.text: Double everything. Double ice cream. Double napkins. Double it. On me.
jokes.setup: Michael planning the party
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top enthusiasm for doubling everything
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Michael's tendency to overcompensate

jokes.text: So I guess some good came out of firing Devin after all. Maybe I should call him, tell him that.
jokes.setup: Michael getting a bonus for firing someone
jokes.punchline: His oblivious suggestion to call the fired employee to share the 'good news'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark comedy showing Michael's complete lack of emotional intelligence and self-awareness

jokes.text: I want people making out in closets. I want people hanging from the ceilings, lampshades on the heads. I want it to be a Playboy Mansion party.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his party vision
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate expectations for an office Christmas party
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Michael's delusional party expectations

jokes.text: Like booze ever killed anybody.
jokes.setup: Can't serve alcohol at the party
jokes.punchline: Michael's obliviously false statement about alcohol safety
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Michael's ability to say completely wrong things with confidence

jokes.text: Will you help me? / No! No way. It... no.
jokes.setup: Jim asking for help with something
jokes.punchline: Dwight's emphatic, repeated refusal
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Jim-Dwight dynamic

jokes.text: That makes sense, because he has elfish features.
jokes.setup: Dwight wanting to be the elf
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact insult disguised as agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - subtle mockery delivered as helpful observation

jokes.text: I got Angela. She's into these posters of babies dressed as adults. I got her one of those. I felt kind of weird buying that.
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining his Secret Santa gift
jokes.punchline: The weirdness of Angela's baby poster obsession and Stanley's discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good character detail for Angela and relatable discomfort from Stanley

jokes.text: I got myself for Secret Santa. I was supposed to tell somebody, but I didn't.
jokes.setup: Creed explaining his Secret Santa situation
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of breaking the system and not caring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - completely breaks social norms with zero self-awareness

jokes.text: Not great. Sorry, everybody. I think the tree looks nice.
jokes.setup: Christmas tree lights failing to work properly
jokes.punchline: Michael's weak attempt to salvage the moment with forced positivity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Michael trying to recover from failure

jokes.text: Presents are the best way to show someone how much you care. It is like this tangible thing that you can point to and say, 'Hey, man, I love you... this many dollars worth.'
jokes.setup: Michael philosophizing about gift-giving
jokes.punchline: Reducing love to a monetary calculation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael moment - takes a sweet sentiment and makes it crassly transactional

jokes.text: He obviously forgot to get me something. And then he went into his closet and dug out this little number. And then threw it in a bag.
jokes.setup: Jim receiving a clearly last-minute gift
jokes.punchline: His detailed, accurate assessment of the gift-giver's laziness
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with Jim's characteristic dry delivery

jokes.text: Yep, that's exactly what happened.
jokes.setup: Jim's accurate description of Roy's lazy gift-giving
jokes.punchline: Roy's matter-of-fact admission without shame
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roy moment - completely unashamed of his laziness

jokes.text: Wasn't there a $20 limit on the gifts? / This is 400 bucks. / You don't know that. / Yeah, you left the price on.
jokes.setup: Michael's expensive iPod gift violating the limit
jokes.punchline: His weak denial followed by the obvious evidence
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation with Michael's terrible attempt to cover his mistake

jokes.text: So Phyllis is basically saying, 'Hey, Michael, I know you did a lot to help the office this year. But I only care about you a homemade oven mitt's worth.' I gave Ryan an iPod.
jokes.setup: Michael receiving a homemade oven mitt
jokes.punchline: His bitter, self-pitying interpretation and petty comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Michael's narcissism and need for validation

jokes.text: We are going to turn Secret Santa into Yankee Swap! / What is Yankee Swap? / I thought that was called Nasty Christmas. / Yeah, we call it White Elephant.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing a rule change
jokes.punchline: Everyone knowing the game by different names
jokes.explanation: Relatable regional differences humor that many viewers can identify with

jokes.text: Michael should have asked the party planning committee first. He's not just supposed to just spring things on us out of nowhere.
jokes.setup: Michael changing the gift exchange rules
jokes.punchline: Angela's rigid adherence to proper procedures for a simple party game
jokes.explanation: Good Angela character moment showing her controlling nature

jokes.text: That was meant for Kelly. / Yeah, I figured.
jokes.setup: Oscar taking the shower radio
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact acknowledgment that he's taking someone else's specific gift
jokes.explanation: Good understated humor about the awkwardness of gift swapping

jokes.text: That's like the creepiest thing that I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Seeing the baby-dressed-as-adult poster
jokes.punchline: Jim's blunt assessment of Angela's weird taste
jokes.explanation: Good reaction humor that validates the audience's likely response

jokes.text: Everyone wants the iPod. It is a huge hit. It is almost a Christmas miracle.
jokes.setup: People stealing the iPod in Yankee Swap
jokes.punchline: Michael's self-congratulatory interpretation of the chaos he created
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment showing his ability to spin any situation as his success

jokes.text: See, I wanted somebody to take it. Boom. Reverse psychology. Reverse psychology is an awesome tool. I don't know if you guys know about it. But, basically, you make someone think the opposite of what you believe. And that tricks them into doing something stupid. Works like a charm.
jokes.setup: Someone taking the oven mitt Michael received
jokes.punchline: Michael's elaborate rationalization and condescending explanation of reverse psychology
jokes.explanation: Great Michael moment - transparent lie delivered with complete confidence and unnecessary explanation

jokes.text: Two paintball lessons with someone as experienced as I am, is worth, easily, like, two grand.
jokes.setup: Dwight defending his paintball lesson gift
jokes.punchline: His completely inflated valuation of his own expertise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment showing his inflated self-regard and bizarre priorities

jokes.text: Maybe I should have taken the iPod.
jokes.setup: Kevin realizing he got a lesser gift in the swap
jokes.punchline: His obvious, understated regret
jokes.explanation: Simple, relatable moment of buyer's remorse

jokes.text: Yankee Swap is like Machiavelli meets... Christmas.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to intellectualize the gift exchange
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-intellectual comparison that doesn't quite work
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment showing his tendency toward fake intellectualism

jokes.text: Well, happy birthday, Jesus. Sorry your party's so lame.
jokes.setup: Michael storming off after party criticism
jokes.punchline: His bitter, blasphemous Christmas comment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael moment - darkly funny, quotable, and perfectly captures his petulant nature

jokes.text: 15 bottles of vodka? Yeah, that should do it.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if it's enough to get 20 people drunk
jokes.punchline: The clerk's casual confirmation of the excessive amount
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor showing Michael's over-the-top solution

jokes.text: A real man makes his own luck. Billy Zane, Titanic.
jokes.setup: Jim offering a shamrock keychain for good luck
jokes.punchline: Dwight's weirdly specific movie quote misattribution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - combines his fake wisdom with bizarre pop culture reference

jokes.text: And sinus infections can be cured by making a tea from green tea leaf stems and pouring it directly into your nose like so.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why he wants the teapot
jokes.punchline: His disgusting and medically dubious use for Pam's special gift
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly gross Dwight moment that makes Jim's romantic gesture absurd

jokes.text: What line of work are you in, Bob?
jokes.setup: Bob has introduced himself multiple times as 'Bob Vance, Vance Refrigeration'
jokes.punchline: Ryan asking what he does despite the obvious repetition
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the running gag - perfectly timed after the repetitive introductions

jokes.text: Kudos to Ryan, king of the party committee!
jokes.setup: Michael praising Ryan's party contribution
jokes.punchline: Ryan explicitly said he did nothing, making Michael's praise absurd
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's favoritism and selective hearing

jokes.text: Not bad... and if it couldn't go to Ryan, you are the guy I'd want it to go to.
jokes.setup: Jim talking to Michael about the teapot gift
jokes.punchline: His backhanded way of acknowledging Michael's Ryan obsession
jokes.explanation: Nice subtle callback that acknowledges Michael's favoritism while being kind

jokes.text: Lampshade on the head! It's happening!
jokes.setup: Someone puts a lampshade on their head
jokes.punchline: Michael's excited recognition of his earlier party vision coming true
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Michael's childlike excitement over achieving his party goals

jokes.text: Christmas is awesome. First of all, you get to spend time with people you love. Secondly, you can get drunk and no one can say anything. Third, you give presents. What's better than giving presents? And fourth, getting presents. So four things. Not bad for one day. It's really the greatest day of all time.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on Christmas
jokes.punchline: His childlike enumeration that includes getting drunk as a Christmas virtue
jokes.explanation: Great Michael moment showing his simplified worldview and misplaced priorities

jokes.text: Ohhhhhhkay. (Meredith's drunken response)
jokes.setup: Meredith clearly very drunk at the end of the party
jokes.punchline: Her slurred, exaggerated response indicating she's wasted
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy payoff to Michael's alcohol plan - Meredith as the office drunk
